steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: $f(m) = 28 - 4m$ gives the account balance after renting $m$ movies. The question asks for the fixed dollar amount withdrawn each time one movie is rented - that is, the magnitude of the rate of change per movie.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A - $4m$: This is an expression that grows with $m$, not a fixed per-rental amount. ✗
  • Choice B - $4$: The coefficient of $m$ is $-4$, meaning the balance drops by $4$ dollars per movie rented. A constant $4$ per rental is correct. ✓
  • Choice C - $28$: This is the initial balance (the $y$-intercept when $m=0$), not the per-rental cost. ✗
  • Choice D - $28 - 4m$: This is the entire expression for the balance, not the amount withdrawn per rental. ✗
B is correct.
